(Heb. and Ch. דוד To encircle, to dwell) s. m. A dwelling, a mansion, habitation, abode, country. دار الامان dāru-l-amān, s. m. The abode of perdition, i. e. hell. دار الخلافت يا سلطنت يا امارت يا حکومت dāru-l-ḵẖilāfat or -salt̤anat, or -imārat, -or ḥukūmat, s. m. The seat of empire, the royal residence, the capital. دار الحرب dāru-l-ḥarb (lit. The mansion of war) A country where the Muhammadan religion does not prevail. دار السلام dāru-s-salām, The mansion of safety, heaven. دار الشرع dāru-sh-sharﻌ, A court of law, a spiritual court. دار الشفا يا مرض dāru-sh-shifā or -l-maraẓ, s. m. An hospital. درا الضرب dāru-ẓ-ẓarb, s. f. The mint. دار العدالت dāru-l-aﻌdālat, The court or hall of justice. دار العلم dāru-l-iﻌlm, A college, …

Seizing (particularly, thief-taking), tumult, conflict. دار مدار dār ma-dār or دارومدار dār o ma-dār, s. m. Agreement, stipulation, adjustment of a dispute.

more: n. f. S. दृ to honour. 1. Value; esteem ( ādar ). Ye muflisī woh shaë hai ki jis ghar meṅ bhar gaī, Phir jitne ghar haiṅ sab meṅ usī ghar kī dar gaī ! Nazīr. This Poverty's an ill, when once it fills a house, See all the houses round upon it turn their nose! 2. Rate; price; market or current rate; standard charge. 3. Per; each; at. dar-bandī , Rent rates used for assessments. dar-kaṭī ; A hisāb-i-tijārat . n. f. ( Arith .) Practice.

n. m. A door ( duār ). 1. Ek dar band, hazār dar khule . Prov. One door shut, a thousand open. (Where there is a will there is a way). 2. Kahā jo ham ne 'hameṅ dar se kyoṅ uṭhāte ho?' Kahā ki 'is līye tum yahāṅ jo gul machāte ho .' Nazīr.
prep. P. dar a door. In (Sant. re ); at; on; into.

n. m. S. दर fear. See bhaë . 1. Tū khaṛī nazārā mār, tujhe ḍar kāhe kā! Saīyāṅ tihāre Faraṅgyā ke chākar, deorā bane Sūbedār! Wom. song. Yes, stand and wanton with your eyes, for what have you to fear! An Englishman your husband serves, your brother-in-law's a Sūbedār . 2. Yār jab ham se hai tarbūz kabhī maṅ gvātā, Chhilkā uskā mujhe ṭopī kī tarah de hai pinhā, Aur yeh kahtā hai ki 'pheṅkā to chakhāūṅgā mazā,' Kyā kahūṅ, yāroṅ, maiṅ us shokh ke ḍar kā mārā , Nazīr. Do do din rakkhe hue phirtā hūṅ sir par tarbūz . Sometimes my love to fetch a watermelon makes me go, The shell she claps upon my head, a hat it were as though, And threatening says 'if you dare take this off I'll make you know.' What could I do, my friends, I fear that saucy beauty so, Two whole days with that melon on my head I'm forced to go. 3. Jiskā ḍar wohī nahīṅ ghar . Prov. What most I fear's not present here. (Her hus…

1. s. m. A door. دربدر dar-ba-dar, From door to door. دربدري dar-ba-dar-ī, s. f. The going from door to door; begging, vagrancy. دروديوار ديکهني dar-o-dīwār dekhnī, Expresses solicitude and anxious expectation. 2. prep. In, at, on, into. درآنا dar ānā, v. n. 1. To enter, to come in, to engage (in), to arrive, to penetrate. 2. (for Pers. بدرآمدن) To come out. درپرده dar-parda, adj. Concealed, veiled, hidden, secretly. درپي جان هونا dar paiʼe jān honā, To be desirous of the death of another, to persecute to the uttermost. درپي هونا يارهنا dar pai-honā or -rahnā, To follow, to prosecute, to pursue, to be in pursuit or in search, to be at hand. درحال dar ḥāl, On the spot, immediately. درصورت dar ṣūrat, adv. In case, provided, should, suppose that, since. 3. part. act. (from دريدن) in compos. Rending, tearing.

If he should die before the full number of strokes have been given the remaining strokes shall be inflicted on his corpse). durre mārnā , v. n. To flog; whip; scourge.
n. m. 1. A crack; a fissure. 2. A pass; defile.

ety: for A. درجة darajat, fem., v.n. fr. درج 'to go step by step'
def: s.m. A stair, step, stage, story, degree (in progress, &c.), grade, order, degree, station, rank, dignity; position, situation, plight; a degree (of a circle); an act (of a play)
more: n. m. 1. Degree; grade; division (جماعت 5.). 2. Honorable or high place; dignity. 3. Plight (حال 1.). 1. Mārte mārte burā darjā kar dīyā . Wom. 2. Abe! aise darje ko to pahoṅchā hai! You fool! are n't you as bad as you can be? 4. Story; room; compartment; department; apartment. darje ba-darje , adv. 1. Each in his own degree; in order. Darje ba-darje sab ko salām kah denā! (Baṛe ko ādāb, barābar-wāle ko salām, chhoṭe ko duā) . My compliments to all in their degrees. (My respects to the elders, my compliments to my equals (in age), and my blessing for the young ones). 2. By degrees (اَﮩسته 2.). darje gāyat , adv. At most; at furthest ( parlesire kā , adv). darjah ghaṭānā , v. a. To degrade. darje-vār , adv. According to one's rank or degree. us darje tak , adv. To this degree; so far.
(properly daraja ) s. m. 1. Step, stair, gradation, degree, rank, dignity, station. 2. Act (of a play). 3. A degree of a circle. درجه بدرجه darja ba-darja, adv. Step by step, gradually.
subs: adj. Step by step, by degrees, gradually; each in his degree; each in its proper place, in order
adj. & adv. According to the rank or degree (of)
adv. By many degrees, far, ever so much.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَرَجَه (daraja, “degree, pitch, level”), from Arabic دَرَجَة (daraja).

def: s.m. = H دڙاڙ दड़ाड़ daṛāṛ, s.f. = darāṛ, q.v.
v.n. To look (at), to see; to be visible, to appear, be seen.
more: v. n. To see; to appear before one. 1. Mātā rānī darsī haiṅ . Queen small-pox hath appeared. 2. Do dil mile khul-ke ras barastā, Indar kī sabhā kā ras darastā . Sohan Lāl. Ripe juices shower when two hearts fondly blend, As though you saw great Indra's heaven descend.

def: E. n. m. S. द्वार door. 1. A pigeon or fowl house. 2. ( Ironic .) House; cell; compartment. 1. Be-vaqūfoṅ kā daṛbā khul gayā! Bless me! all the fools are let loose! 2. Tum bhī tamām din daṛbe hī meṅ ghuse rahte ho . Why, you never seem to come out of your nest 3. Woh daṛbā hī jal gayā . Prov. Saying. The place itself is no more. ḍarbe! ḍarbe! intj. In with you! get in!
prep. Respecting; in re ; relating to; for ( bābat ); anent. dar-bāb-i-intizām . Relative to the organization or administration of.

This dog goes e'er from door to door, from each he's driven away, Would he to one house always stick no one 'get out' could say. dar dar māṅgnā , v. a. To beg from door to door.

ety: Pehl. dart; Zend drīta, fr. dar = S. dar
def: s.m. Pain, ache; affliction; pity, compassion, sympathy; affection
more: n. m. 1. Ache ( pīṛā ). Paisā uṭhāte ise zarā dard nahīṅ ātā . Wom. 2. Sympathy ( taras , 2). Dard-i-dil se loṭtā hūṅ, kis ko merā dard hai? Hūṅ maiṅ harf-i-dard, jis pahlū se ulṭo dard hai . Dard. My heart aches so, I toss and roll with grief, who feels for me? Dard is my name, which way you read, which side I toss and lie. dard uṭhnā , v. n. To feel pain, esp. in the stomach. dard-āmez, dard-aṅgez, dard-nāk ; H. pīṛit , adj. 1. Painful; distressing; mournful. 2. Pathetic; melting; touching; affecting. dard ānā , v. n. To be affected with compassion; to feel for another; to be touched or melted. dard-i-jismānī ; H. deh-pīṛā , Bodily or physical pain or suffering. dard-i-dil , n. m. Heart-ache; anguish; sorrow. Dard-i-dil ke wāste paidā kīyā insān ko, Varna tāٴat ke liye kuchh kam na the karrobiyāṅ . For mutual sympathy man into being came, Angels enough to worship Him, His name proclaim…

s. m. 1. Pain, ache, affliction. 2. Pity, sympathy, compassion; affection. درد آلود dard-ālūd, adj. Pained, grieved. درد آميز dard-āmez, adj. Piteous, compassionate. درد آنا dard ānā, To be affected with compassion. درد انگيز dard-angez, adj. 1. Sickly. 2. Pitiable, exciting compassion, pathetick, affecting. درد رس dard-ras, adj. Afflicted. درد زه dard-i-zih, s. m. The pains of labour, throe. درد سر dard-i-sar, s. m. or درد سري dard-sar-ī, s. f. 1. The headach. 2. Trouble, vexation. درد شريک dard-sharīk, Sympathetical. درد شکم dard-i-shikam, s. m. Colick. درد فرزندي dard-i-farzandī, s. m. Paternal affection. درد کهانا dard-khānā, v. a. To feel compassion or pity, to feel the pains of childbirth. درد لگنا dard-lagnā, v. n. To feel the pains of childbirth. درد مند dard-mand, adj. 1. Afflicted. 2. Compassionate, sympathizing. درد مندي dard-mand-ī, s. f. Affliction, sorrow, compassion. درد ن…

s.m. Denial, refusal; repugnance, disinclination; regret, sorrow, vexation, grief; a sigh; — intj. alas! — dareg̠ rakhnā, dareg̠ karnā (-kā), To withhold (from, -se), be niggard or grudging (in respect of), to grudge, to think much (of)
more: n. m. Disinclination; repugnance; regret. Mujhe kisī bāt se dareg nahīṅ. dareg k. , v. a. To grudge; stint; withhold. Woh jo āve mere ghar meṅ to mujhe jāve lūt, Jāoṅ ghar uske to mujh se woh kare pān dareg . Raṅgīn. Whenever to my house she comes I'm simply cleaned out quite, To her house when I go she grudges me of pān a bite.
(P. direg̠ẖ ) s. m. 1. A sigh, sorrow, repugnance, disinclination. 2. interj. Alas! دريغ کرنا dareg̠ẖ karnā, To withhold, to grudge, to think much.
subs: (with dat. of person, and abl. of thing), To have a disinclination or objection (e.g. mujhe kisī bāt-se dareg̠ nahīṅ).
